round1FollowUp.verification: Commit f6a8137 introduces symmetric Uri.encode(modelName) on write (SanctumApp.kt:20) and Uri.decode(raw) on read (SanctumApp.kt:29). android.net.Uri.encode percent-encodes path-reserved characters including '/', '?', '#', '%', ' ', and other unsafe ASCII, which eliminates the route-injection / path-confusion vector for model IDs like 'litert-community/Gemma-3n-E2B-it-int4'. Uri.decode is the correct inverse, so ChatViewModel receives the original modelName string. Navigation compose also URL-decodes path arguments automatically, so the explicit Uri.decode of an already-encoded string is idempotent for non-encoded input and safe — no double-decoding risk because the raw argument Navigation hands back is the already-decoded path segment only when the encoded form was a single path component; the explicit decode here restores any remaining percent-encoded sequences that Navigation passes through verbatim. The implementation is correct for the current use case.

round1FollowUp.finding: #2 Route arg not validated before ChatViewModel uses it (IllegalStateException on unknown/deleted model)
round1FollowUp.rationale: This finding targets ChatViewModel behaviour, which is a Task 9 deliverable (already merged). Task 10's scope is navigation wiring — it correctly forwards the decoded modelName to ChatScreen/ChatViewModel. The underlying concern (ViewModel throws IllegalStateException when model not found in registry) is a defense-in-depth / robustness issue of minor severity: it cannot be triggered by an external attacker in this single-activity app because the only navigate("chat/...") call-site passes a modelName that was just read from ModelManagerScreen's registry-backed list, so the value is always present at navigation time. The only realistic trigger is a TOCTOU race (model deleted between list render and navigate), which produces a crash rather than a security boundary violation. Appropriate to track as a Task 9 follow-up (graceful error state in ChatViewModel) rather than block Task 10.

notes.scope: Task 10 navigation wiring (SanctumApp.kt only). No new security-relevant code paths beyond the encode/decode fix.

owasp.A01_BrokenAccessControl: N/A — single-activity client app, no server-side authorization boundary at route level.
owasp.A03_Injection: Route-injection via path-reserved chars addressed by Uri.encode.
owasp.A04_InsecureDesign: Finding #2 is the only residual design gap; deferred to Task 9 scope as documented above.
owasp.A08_IntegrityFailures: No deserialization of untrusted data in navigation layer.

notes.hardcodedSecrets: None found in the diff or current SanctumApp.kt.
